info.title: Ontology Platform MVP6.6 Governance Change Application Planning API
info.description: Planning-only OpenAPI artifact for MVP6.6 Governance Change Application: the deferred application slice from ADR 0012, fixed durably by ADR 0013. A privileged human explicitly APPLIES an APPROVED + application_state=QUEUED ontology change request onto a DRAFT ontology version (QUEUED -> APPLIED), executing the request's change items via the existing MVP1 ontology-edit semantics (ADD=create, MODIFY=update, DEPRECATE=archive) on a DRAFT version ONLY. APPLICATION != PUBLISH: apply never touches the published graph, never starts a publish job, never rolls back; publishing stays the separate MVP3 path. There is no auto-apply (approval never triggers apply). Staleness is auto-detected at apply -> 409 CHANGE_REQUEST_SUPERSEDED + application_state=SUPERSEDED (no mutation). Idempotency: already-applied -> 409 CHANGE_ALREADY_APPLIED; not APPROVED/QUEUED -> 409 CHANGE_NOT_APPLICABLE; non-DRAFT target -> 409 APPLY_TARGET_NOT_DRAFT. Apply rights = approver rights (ONTOLOGY_MANAGER/PROJECT_ADMIN/SYSTEM_ADMIN); applier may differ from approver; unauthorized -> 403 PERMISSION_DENIED. The successful-apply response carries GovernanceApplicationMutationGuard with exactly ONE true flag (ontology_draft_mutated); read/precheck/blocked-apply keep the all-false GovernanceMutationGuard. Additive/disjoint to MVP1-MVP6.5: reuses MVP6.5 governance change-request/item/GovernanceApplicationState/audit shapes, MVP1 ontology-version + OntologyElementStatus/OntologyVersionStatus, and MVP5 Role by reference (no renames). Wave43 contract-first planning only: no runtime code, models, migrations, or tests. Frozen by docs/pm/MVP6_6_GOVERNANCE_APPLICATION_BRIEF.md and docs/adr/0013.

tags.name: MVP6.6 Governance Application
tags.description: Additive human-initiated application of an APPROVED+QUEUED change request onto a DRAFT ontology version. Application != publish (draft-only). The successful apply response carries GovernanceApplicationMutationGuard with exactly one true flag (ontology_draft_mutated); the application-status pre-check and application-audit reads, and every blocked apply, keep the all-false GovernanceMutationGuard. Apply valid only from APPROVED + application_state=QUEUED; apply rights = ONTOLOGY_MANAGER/PROJECT_ADMIN/SYSTEM_ADMIN.

get.description: Read-only pre-check for the apply action. Resolves the effective target DRAFT ontology version (explicit target_ontology_version_id query param, else the project current DRAFT), reports per-item before/after element refs and a staleness/would-supersede hint, and returns the apply capability + guard (all-false GovernanceMutationGuard: reads never mutate). This read NEVER flips QUEUED->SUPERSEDED; only the apply attempt is authoritative. The hint is advisory: a request the pre-check marks would_supersede=true still transitions only on the apply call.

post.summary: Apply an APPROVED+QUEUED change request to a DRAFT ontology version
post.description: Human-initiated apply. Valid ONLY when status==APPROVED AND application_state==QUEUED. Executes the change items against a DRAFT ontology version via MVP1 ontology-edit semantics (ADD=create, MODIFY=update, DEPRECATE=set OntologyElementStatus=ARCHIVED), then sets application_state=APPLIED and returns GovernanceApplicationMutationGuard with ontology_draft_mutated=true (all others false). Target = optional target_ontology_version_id (must be OntologyVersionStatus=DRAFT) else the project current DRAFT; non-DRAFT -> 409 APPLY_TARGET_NOT_DRAFT. Idempotent: already-APPLIED -> 409 CHANGE_ALREADY_APPLIED; not APPROVED/QUEUED -> 409 CHANGE_NOT_APPLICABLE. Staleness auto-detected at apply time -> block, mutate nothing, application_state=SUPERSEDED, 409 CHANGE_REQUEST_SUPERSEDED. Apply rights = ONTOLOGY_MANAGER/PROJECT_ADMIN/SYSTEM_ADMIN (= approver rights; applier may differ from approver/proposer; no self-apply prohibition); unauthorized -> 403 PERMISSION_DENIED. NEVER touches the published graph, starts a publish/extraction/evaluation job, or rolls back. Writes a CHANGE_REQUEST_APPLIED application audit entry with per-item before/after element refs and the resulting draft version id.

200.description: Applied to the DRAFT ontology version (application_state=APPLIED). Response guard ontology_draft_mutated=true; all others false.

get.summary: Read the application audit trail for a change request
get.description: Read-only application audit for a change request: CHANGE_REQUEST_APPLIED / CHANGE_REQUEST_SUPERSEDED entries with actor+role, timestamp, source request id + applied item ids, resulting DRAFT target_ontology_version_id, and per-item before/after element refs. All-false GovernanceMutationGuard implied (read). Paginated (limit/cursor), chronological ASC.

TargetOntologyVersionIdQuery.name: target_ontology_version_id
TargetOntologyVersionIdQuery.description: Optional explicit DRAFT ontology version id to pre-check against. Omit to use the project current DRAFT.

ApplicationActionFilter.name: action
ApplicationActionFilter.description: Filter application audit entries by action

NotFound.description: Resource not found (404: PROJECT_NOT_FOUND / CHANGE_REQUEST_NOT_FOUND / ONTOLOGY_VERSION_NOT_FOUND)

ApplyConflict.description: Apply blocked (409). CHANGE_ALREADY_APPLIED (already APPLIED), CHANGE_NOT_APPLICABLE (not APPROVED/QUEUED), CHANGE_REQUEST_SUPERSEDED (stale; application_state now SUPERSEDED, nothing mutated), APPLY_TARGET_NOT_DRAFT (target not OntologyVersionStatus=DRAFT). Every blocked apply mutates nothing; if a body is returned its mutation_guard is all-false.

GovernanceApplicationState.description: Reused verbatim from MVP6.5 (docs/api/openapi-mvp6-5-draft.json), NOT renamed. Orthogonal post-approval intent lifecycle. QUEUED on APPROVE (intent only). MVP6.6 is the FIRST slice that produces APPLIED (successful apply) and SUPERSEDED (staleness block); both were reserved in ADR 0012. SUPERSEDED is terminal for application.

ChangeRequestChangeType.description: Reused verbatim from MVP6.5, NOT renamed. Drives the applied ontology-edit semantics on a DRAFT version: ADD=create element, MODIFY=update element, DEPRECATE=set OntologyElementStatus=ARCHIVED.

ChangeRequestTargetKind.description: Reused verbatim from MVP6.5, NOT renamed. The ontology element kind an applied change item targets.

OntologyElementStatus.description: Reused verbatim from apps/backend/app/core/enums.py, NOT renamed / redefined; mirrored here so this planning artifact is standalone. DEPRECATE applies to ARCHIVED; apply never hard-deletes (DELETED is not produced by apply).

OntologyVersionStatus.description: Reused verbatim from apps/backend/app/core/enums.py, NOT renamed / redefined. Apply targets a DRAFT version ONLY; PUBLISHED/ARCHIVED targets -> 409 APPLY_TARGET_NOT_DRAFT.

Role.description: Reused verbatim from apps/backend/app/core/enums.py (NOT renamed / redefined; mirrored here so this planning artifact is standalone). Apply rights = approver rights: ONTOLOGY_MANAGER/PROJECT_ADMIN/SYSTEM_ADMIN. No distinct apply role literal is introduced.

GovernanceApplicationAuditAction.description: New MVP6.6 audit actions naming application events. Additive; does NOT rename MVP6.5 GovernanceAuditAction or MVP3/MVP5 AuditEventType. CHANGE_REQUEST_APPLIED on a successful apply; CHANGE_REQUEST_SUPERSEDED on a staleness block.

GovernanceApplicationMutationGuard.description: REDEFINED guard for the SUCCESSFUL apply response only (ADR 0013). Exactly ONE flag is legitimately true: ontology_draft_mutated. All other flags are false. Any other true flag, or a true ontology_draft_mutated on any non-apply endpoint, is a defect. Blocked apply (idempotency/staleness/authz) mutates nothing and uses the all-false GovernanceMutationGuard instead.

ontology_draft_mutated.description: The ONE sanctioned mutation surface: a DRAFT ontology version only.

published_graph_mutated.description: Publish stays the separate MVP3 path.

GovernanceMutationGuard.description: Reused verbatim from MVP6.5 (NOT renamed). All-false. Carried by the application-status pre-check read, the application-audit read, and EVERY blocked apply (idempotency/staleness/authz), which mutate nothing. Only the successful apply uses the redefined GovernanceApplicationMutationGuard.

OntologyElementRef.description: A reference to a single ontology element the apply touches. Exactly one of the id fields is set, matching target_kind. status is the element's OntologyElementStatus at the referenced moment (before or after).

ApplicationItemPreview.description: Per-change-item before/after preview for the pre-check. before_ref is null for ADD (no prior element). after_ref describes the intended post-apply element. stale=true when the item's approved before-state no longer matches the current draft target (see stale_reason).

stale.description: Advisory only; the authoritative staleness transition happens on the apply attempt.

ApplicationCapabilities.description: Read-only permission + eligibility hint for the UI. Display-only; the server enforces authorization and preconditions independently and this never widens the boundary. can_apply is true only when the actor holds apply rights AND status==APPROVED AND application_state==QUEUED.

GovernanceApplicationStatusResponse.description: Read-only apply pre-check. Resolves the effective DRAFT target, per-item before/after preview, and a would_supersede staleness hint. Never mutates; mutation_guard is all-false. This read NEVER flips QUEUED->SUPERSEDED.

status.description: MVP6.5 OntologyChangeRequestStatus of the request (e.g. APPROVED).

target_ontology_version_id.description: Resolved effective DRAFT target (explicit query param or project current DRAFT). Null if no DRAFT version exists.

target_version_status.description: Status of the resolved target; must be DRAFT for apply to be eligible.

target_is_draft.description: True iff the resolved target is OntologyVersionStatus=DRAFT.

applicable.description: True iff status==APPROVED AND application_state==QUEUED AND target_is_draft AND not would_supersede.

would_supersede.description: Advisory staleness hint: apply would be blocked and the request superseded. Authoritative only on the apply attempt.

GovernanceApplyRequest.description: Optional apply body. target_ontology_version_id selects the DRAFT version to receive the change; omit to use the project current DRAFT. note is an optional operator note recorded on the application audit entry.

target_ontology_version_id.description: DRAFT ontology version id to apply into. Must be OntologyVersionStatus=DRAFT (else 409 APPLY_TARGET_NOT_DRAFT). Omit for project current DRAFT.

note.description: Optional operator note recorded on the application audit entry.

GovernanceApplicationAuditEntry.description: Application audit entry. Reuses the MVP3/MVP5 audit shape (actor_id, action, reason/note, timestamp) by reference; action is GovernanceApplicationAuditAction. Records the resulting DRAFT target_ontology_version_id, applied item ids, and per-item before/after element refs so the exact draft mutation is reconstructable. Never hard-deleted.

target_ontology_version_id.description: Resulting DRAFT version id on APPLIED; the attempted target on SUPERSEDED.

stale_detail.description: On CHANGE_REQUEST_SUPERSEDED, the mismatch detail (which items/elements were stale and why).

ApplicationBeforeAfterRef.description: Per-item before/after element ref pair for a successful apply. before is null for ADD; after is null for a DEPRECATE that archived (after status ARCHIVED) is still populated with the archived ref.

GovernanceApplyResponse.description: Successful apply envelope. application_state is APPLIED. mutation_guard is the redefined GovernanceApplicationMutationGuard with ontology_draft_mutated=true (all others false). Carries the resulting DRAFT target_ontology_version_id, the application audit entry, and per-item before/after refs.

target_ontology_version_id.description: The DRAFT version that received the change.

ApiError.description: Standard error. codes: PROJECT_NOT_FOUND, CHANGE_REQUEST_NOT_FOUND, ONTOLOGY_VERSION_NOT_FOUND, APPLY_TARGET_NOT_DRAFT (409), CHANGE_ALREADY_APPLIED (409), CHANGE_NOT_APPLICABLE (409), CHANGE_REQUEST_SUPERSEDED (409), PERMISSION_DENIED (403).
